channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: F.B.I. Patrols Neighborhood of Man Held in Connection to Gala Shooting
item.description: The F.B.I was seen at the home of a man held in connection with the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner. Residents of Torrance, Calif. said they were surprised to hear that the suspect appeared to be from their neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 27, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.description: tktkt
item.dc:creator: Kentaro Takahashi

item.title: Eran la ‘segunda familia’ de Michael Jackson. Ahora dicen que abusó de ellos
item.description: Tras haberlo apoyado durante años ante las acusaciones de abuso sexual infantil, los hermanos Cascio han demandado al patrimonio de Michael Jackson.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens

media:content.media:description: The Cascio siblings: from left, Dominic, Aldo, Marie Nicole and Eddie.

item.title: Word of the Day: calumny
item.description: This word has appeared in eight articles on NYTimes.com in the past two years. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ‘Widow’s Bay,’ and 6 More Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: The Apple TV show starring Matthew Rhys premieres, and two true crime documentaries air.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: Matthew Rhys in “Widow’s Bay.”

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Another Boat in Eastern Pacific, Killing 3
item.description: The United States has carried out dozens of boat strikes since September against people the Trump administration accuses of smuggling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young

media:content.media:description: An image from a video provided by the U.S. military shows what it said was a strike on a boat in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Sunday.

item.title: Trump pretende abolir las reservas atómicas de Irán, un problema que él ayudó a crear
item.description: El presidente Trump se retiró del acuerdo nuclear de la era Obama en 2018. Pero Irán respondió con una oleada de enriquecimiento que tensa las negociaciones actuales.
item.dc:creator: William J. Broad and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: En febrero, como preparación para una posible guerra con Estados Unidos, Irán trasladó lanzamisiles a posiciones situadas a corta distancia de las fuerzas militares israelíes y estadounidenses.

item.title: Can King Charles Help Heal the U.S.-British Rupture?
item.description: Not since his mother, Queen Elizabeth II, traveled to Washington after the Suez Crisis has a visit by the British monarch come at such a fraught time in Anglo-American relations.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Elizabeth II, Queen of Great Britain

media:content.media:description: King Charles III, center, and President Trump with a Coldstream Guardsman in September at Windsor Castle.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Confusion and Fright Among Guests
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, April 27.

item.title: 8 Memorable Moments From Past British Royal Visits to the U.S.
item.description: With King Charles III and Queen Camilla set to arrive for a state visit with President Trump, we look back at some major moments in previous royal trips to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Esther Bintliff and Sarah Eckinger
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Elizabeth II, Queen of Great Britain
George VI, King of Great Britain

media:content.media:description: Queen Elizabeth and President Ronald Reagan in San Francisco, in 1983.

item.title: The Rising Chinese Automaker Not Named BYD
item.description: Geely is challenging the giant BYD by adapting quickly to swings in demand and energy prices, seizing on interest in electric vehicles prompted by the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times

item.title: No Corrections: April 27, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, April 27, 2026.

item.title: California Billionaire Tax Has Enough Signatures to Land on Ballot, Backers Say
item.description: The measure calls for placing a one-time 5 percent tax on the assets of California residents with at least $1.1 billion. Opponents are backing competing measures to counter the tax.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The billionaire tax’s supporters, who have been gathering signatures for months, promoted the measure at a booth at the California Democratic Convention in November.

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 6 Recap: Everything Breaks
item.description: Valerie has been terrible at keeping the A.I. thing a secret. Almost as terrible as Billy is at being her manager.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray

media:content.media:description: Valerie (Lisa Kudrow) is in damage control mode after spilling the A.I. beans once again.

item.title: ‘The Lost Boys’ Review: Live, Die, Reprise
item.description: A Broadway musical adaptation of the 1987 movie gets a lot of mileage from ’80s rocker aesthetics and over-the-top spectacle — until its second half.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw and Sara Krulwich

item.title: Así es como Nieves González saltó a la fama tras retratar a Lily Allen
item.description: Nieves González, una pintora de 29 años, trabajaba antes en un relativo anonimato en Andalucía. Retratar a la cantante británica Lily Allen cambió eso.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz

media:content.media:credit: Finbarr O'Reilly for The New York Times

item.title: Muted Reaction as Oil and Stock Markets Reopen
item.description: Oil prices rose and stocks fell after President Trump called off a trip to Pakistan by two U.S. negotiators for peace talks with Iran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Dinner, Interrupted: Over Brunch, Journalists Reflect on a Chaotic Night
item.description: Moving to a new venue, or the East Wing? Plans for the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner are fluid following Saturday’s shocking events.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The scene at the start of the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner on Saturday night.

item.title: Gala Shooting Suspect Was Targeting Trump Administration Officials, Writings Reveal
item.description: A man being held in connection with the attack wrote a note indicating potential targets and grievances, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin and Chelsia Rose Marcius

media:content.media:description: A cameraman stands in front of a California home connected to Cole Tomas Allen, who is being held in connection to an attack at a gala in Washington, D.C., according to officials.

item.title: What We Know About the Gunman at the White House Press Dinner
item.description: The authorities are investigating a motive for the attack by the suspect, identified as Cole Tomas Allen. Those who know him described him as a “completely average guy.”
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri and Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: A police officer blocked a street in Torrance, Calif., as F.B.I. agents investigated a residence linked to the suspect on Saturday night.

item.title: Security at Correspondents’ Dinner Worked as Intended, Experts Say
item.description: Officials noted that the suspect never made it into the hotel ballroom, where President Trump and hundreds of journalists were gathered for the White House correspondents’ dinner.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett, Eileen Sullivan and Chelsia Rose Marcius

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The scene in the hallways after shots were fired during the 2026 White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner at the Washington Hilton on Saturday.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 27, 2026
item.description: Ease into your solving week with a Neville Fogarty puzzle that’s very relaxing.
item.dc:creator: Nikita Richardson

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Vanni for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Need a hint? Take your pick!

item.title: ‘Shots Fired!’: Inside the Pandemonium at the Washington Hilton
item.description: Guests dived to the floor and took cover as Secret Service agents climbed over tables to protect some of the country’s most high-ranking officials, including President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Shawn McCreesh, Tyler Pager and Maggie Haberman

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times

item.title: Map: 6.1-Magnitude Earthquake Shakes Northern Japan
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

item.title: How a Trump Event Shooting Unfolded
item.description: The gunman had written a “manifesto,” the president said, before trying to enter a dinner in Washington for the White House press corps.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times

item.title: After Correspondents Dinner Shooting, Rumors and Conspiracy Theories Swirl Online
item.description: Influencers jumped to fill the information void with conspiracy theories about the attack at the White House Correspondents’ dinner on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Stuart A. Thompson, Tiffany Hsu and Steven Lee Myers

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Security outside the Washington Hilton after gun shots were fired during the 2026 White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner Saturday night.

item.title: Most Guests Ducked for Cover. This Man Munched on His Burrata Salad.
item.description: Michael Glantz, a top agent at Creative Artists Agency, calmly ate his appetizer during the commotion at the White House correspondents’ dinner. He had his reasons.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager

item.title: Hotel Where Reagan Was Shot in 1981 Becomes a Crime Scene Again
item.description: The Washington Hilton was the site of an assassination attempt against the 40th president.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: Secret Service agents shoved President Ronald Reagan into his limousine after he was shot in Washington on March 30, 1981.

item.title: Former Israeli Premiers Join in Bid to Oust Netanyahu in Elections
item.description: Naftali Bennett, a right-wing politician, and Yair Lapid, a centrist, will merge parties for a vote later this year.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner

media:content.media:description: Naftali Bennett, left, and Yair Lapid on Sunday in Herzliya, Israel, where they said they would unite in a party to be called Yachad, Hebrew for “together.”

item.title: Powerful Storm Hits North Texas and Kills 2 as Thousands Remain Without Power
item.description: Thousands remain without power as extensive damage occurred across two counties northwest of Fort Worth.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam

media:content.media:description: At least 11,000 people remain without power, according to Jason Lane, the emergency management coordinator for Parker County.

item.title: What Our Reporter Saw During the D.C. Shooting
item.description: Our reporter was with President Trump at the White House correspondents’ dinner when a gunman breached security. He describes the frantic scenes that unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh and Nikolay Nikolov

item.title: What to Know About U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: An Iranian negotiator returned to Pakistan on Sunday, despite the United States abruptly calling off a trip there by its two top negotiators.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Posters highlighting talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Saturday.

item.title: Family Detained for Months at Texas ICE Facility Released
item.description: Two days after their release, the mother and her five children were again detained during an ICE check-in. A judge halted their removal and returned the family home.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: Hayam El Gamal and her five children spent nearly a year in an immigration detention facility in Dilley, Texas.

item.title: Trump habla sobre su estado mental después de una noche de caos
item.description: La respuesta del presidente de Estados Unidos a la noche del sábado fue extraordinariamente serena para alguien que ha sobrevivido a dos intentos de asesinato.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump speaking in the White House briefing room on Saturday night.

item.title: Social Media Captures the Chaos and Mundane of Shooting at Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: Journalists and guests shared the surreal and confusing scenes from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner after gunfire erupted on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The 2026 White House Correspondents’ Association dinner at the Washington Hilton after gunfire on Saturday night.

item.title: For Oz Pearlman, a Correspondents’ Dinner Not Even He Could Have Predicted
item.description: The mentalist was the featured entertainer at the White House Correspondents’ dinner. He was in the middle of a trick when gunshots rang out.
item.dc:creator: Adam Skolnick

media:content.media:credit: Pete Kiehart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Oz Pearlman was the featured entertainer at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner.

item.title: White House Correspondents’ Dinner Is a Washington Institution
item.description: The dinner, a celebration of press freedom and the First Amendment, has been held annually for more than 50 years. It is both loved and loathed.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins

media:content.media:description: President Franklin D. Roosevelt speaking at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in 1941.

item.title: After the Gunfire, the Parties Went On, Uneasily
item.description: Hosts in the nation’s capital decided to carry on in the wake of a violent incident at the White House correspondents’ dinner.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Andrew Trunsky

media:content.media:credit: Pete Kiehart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Guests at a party hosted by the newsletter platform Substack on Saturday night reacting to news of gunfire at the White House correspondents’ dinner.

item.title: Review: Yunchan Lim Warms Up for Graduation at Carnegie Hall
item.description: This 22-year-old superstar pianist, who is still a student, tested out his graduation recital in New York before taking it to school in Boston.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Barone

item.title: Trump Praises WHCA President Weijia Jiang After Shooting at Dinner
item.description: Weijia Jiang, a CBS correspondent, had been sitting next to the president at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner when shots rang out. Soon afterward, she was reporting on the attack.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg

media:content.media:description: The CBS correspondent Weijia Jiang, the president of the White House Correspondents’ Association, at the start of the group’s annual dinner on Saturday.

item.title: Tiroteo en la cena de corresponsales de la Casa Blanca: esto es lo que sabemos
item.description: Un hombre que portaba varias armas intentó irrumpir en la cena a la que asistía el presidente Trump. La policía detuvo a un sospechoso.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un agente de las fuerzas del orden se colocó en posición después de que se oyeron disparos en el Washington Hilton el sábado.

item.title: After Shooting, Trump Demands Approval for His White House Ballroom
item.description: An incident involving an armed man at the White House correspondents’ dinner has the president to renewing his push for a project slowed by litigation.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Ali Watkins and Zach Montague

media:content.media:description: The construction site for the new White House ballroom last month.

item.title: I’m in Quiche Mode
item.description: And, with these steps for composing the parts ahead of time, you can easily pull together a classic quiche Lorraine for breakfast, lunch or dinner.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Ko

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Vanni for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Spencer Richards.
media:content.media:description: Craig Claiborne’s quiche Lorraine.

item.title: Shooting Prompts Discussions About King Charles III and Queen Camilla’s U.S. Visit
item.description: Buckingham Palace said the royal visit by King Charles III and Queen Camilla that was set to begin on Monday would proceed as planned.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla are expected to begin a state visit this week, including stops in Washington, D.C., and New York.

item.title: Iran and U.S. Sink Into Awkward Limbo of ‘No War, No Peace’
item.description: Each side is betting it can last longer than the other, analysts say. But there are risks in a stalemate without a deal.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A mural in Tehran depicting Iranian missiles attacking a U.S. Navy ship.

item.title: Correspondents’ Dinner Suspect Appears to Have Targeted Trump Officials, Blanche Says
item.description: “We know there were some writings,” the acting attorney general told “Meet the Press.” He said the review of the evidence was still in its early stages.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, spoke at the White House on Saturday after the shooting.

item.title: Puzzle Contest: Letters to the Editors
item.description: Solve all five puzzles for a chance to win.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Thom Tillis Is Prepared to Advance Kevin Warsh After U.S. Drops Fed Inquiry
item.description: Senator Thom Tillis said he had received assurances from federal prosecutors that eased his concerns, setting the stage for a key committee vote on Kevin Warsh.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Colby Smith

media:content.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Thom Tillis in the Capitol this month.

item.title: The Tears at ‘Death of a Salesman’
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay by Eric Alterman, about the power of the Arthur Miller play. Also: Patients and chatbots.

media:content.media:credit: Cecil Beaton/Condé Nast, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Lee J. Cobb, left, Mildred Dunnock and Arthur Kennedy in the first Broadway production of “Death of a Salesman,” in 1949.
